items.title: Lexical scope and temporal dead zone: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? JavaScript resolves a variable from whichever caller function invoked the current function.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. JavaScript uses lexical scope: name resolution follows where functions and blocks are defined, not their caller.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. JavaScript uses lexical scope: name resolution follows where functions and blocks are defined, not their caller.
